intro: Orthochilus coddii, also known as Codd's Orthochilus, is a species of orchid that is native to the mountainous regions of South Africa. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Orthochilus coddii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin conditions and wound healing.
